speaker: OTHELLO
text: It is the cause, it is the cause, my soul.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let me not name it to you, you chaste stars.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It is the cause. Yet I’ll not shed her blood,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nor scar that whiter skin of hers than snow,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And smooth as monumental alabaster.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yet she must die, else she’ll betray more men.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Put out the light, and then put out the light.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If I quench thee, thou flaming minister,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I can again thy former light restore

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Should I repent me. But once put out thy light,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thou cunning’st pattern of excelling nature,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I know not where is that Promethean heat

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That can thy light relume. When I have plucked the rose,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I cannot give it vital growth again.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It needs must wither. I’ll smell it on the tree.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O balmy breath, that dost almost persuade

speaker: OTHELLO
text:
Justice to break her sword!
                 
                He
                 
                kisses
                 
                her
                .
               One more, one more.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Be thus when thou art dead, and I will kill thee

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And love thee after. One more, and this the last.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: So sweet was ne’er so fatal. I must weep,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But they are cruel tears. This sorrow’s heavenly:

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It strikes where it doth love. She wakes.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Who’s there? Othello?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, Desdemona.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Will you come to bed, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Have you prayed tonight, Desdemon?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Ay, my lord.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If you bethink yourself of any crime

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Unreconciled as yet to heaven and grace,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Solicit for it straight.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Alack, my lord,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What may you mean by that?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Well, do it, and be brief. I will walk by.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I would not kill thy unpreparèd spirit.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: No, heaven forfend! I would not kill thy soul.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Talk you of killing?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, I do.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Then heaven have mercy on me.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Amen, with all my heart.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If you say so, I hope you will not kill me.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Humh.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And yet I fear you, for you’re fatal then

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: When your eyes roll so. Why I should fear I know not,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Since guiltiness I know not, but yet I feel I fear.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Think on thy sins.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: They are loves I bear to you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, and for that thou diest.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: That death’s unnatural that kills for loving.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Alas, why gnaw you so your nether lip?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Some bloody passion shakes your very frame.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: These are portents, but yet I hope, I hope

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: They do not point on me.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Peace, and be still.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I will so. What’s the matter?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That handkerchief

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Which I so loved, and gave thee, thou gav’st to Cassio.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: No, by my life and soul! Send for the man

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And ask him.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Sweet soul, take heed, take heed of perjury.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thou art on thy deathbed.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Ay, but not yet to die.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yes, presently.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Therefore confess thee freely of thy sin.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For to deny each article with oath

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Cannot remove nor choke the strong conception

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That I do groan withal. Thou art to die.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Then Lord have mercy on me!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I say “Amen.”

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And have you mercy, too. I never did

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Offend you in my life, never loved Cassio

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But with such general warranty of heaven

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: As I might love. I never gave him token.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: By heaven, I saw my handkerchief in ’s hand!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O perjured woman, thou dost stone my heart

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And mak’st me call what I intend to do

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A murder, which I thought a sacrifice!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I saw the handkerchief!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: He found it, then.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I never gave it him. Send for him hither.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Let him confess a truth.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: He hath confessed.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That he hath used thee.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: How? Unlawfully?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: He will not say so.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: No. His mouth is stopped.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Honest Iago hath ta’en order for ’t.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: O, my fear interprets. What, is he dead?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Had all his hairs been lives, my great revenge

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Had stomach for them all.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Alas, he is betrayed, and I undone.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Out, strumpet! Weep’st thou for him to my face?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: O banish me, my lord, but kill me not!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Down, strumpet!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Kill me tomorrow, let me live tonight.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nay, if you strive—

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But half an hour!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Being done, there is no pause.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But while I say one prayer!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It is too late.

speaker: EMILIA
text: My lord, my lord? What ho! My lord, my lord!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What noise is this?—Not dead? Not yet quite dead?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I that am cruel am yet merciful.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I would not have thee linger in thy pain.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: So, so.

speaker: EMILIA
text: What, ho! My lord, my lord!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Who’s there?

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, good my lord, I would speak a word with you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yes. ’Tis Emilia.—By and by.—She’s dead.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis like she comes to speak of Cassio’s death.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The noise was here. Ha, no more moving?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Still as the grave. Shall she come in? Were ’t good?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I think she stirs again. No. What’s best to do?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If she come in, she’ll sure speak to my wife.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: My wife, my wife! What wife? I have no wife.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O insupportable! O heavy hour!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Methinks it should be now a huge eclipse

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of sun and moon, and that th’ affrighted globe

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Should yawn at alteration.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I do beseech you

speaker: EMILIA
text: That I may speak with you. O, good my lord—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I had forgot thee. O, come in, Emilia.—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Soft, by and by, let me the curtains draw.—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Where art thou?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What’s the matter with thee now?

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, my good lord, yonder’s foul murders done.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What? Now?

speaker: EMILIA
text: But now, my lord.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It is the very error of the moon.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She comes more nearer Earth than she was wont

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And makes men mad.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Cassio, my lord, hath killed

speaker: EMILIA
text: A young Venetian called Roderigo.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Roderigo killed? And Cassio killed?

speaker: EMILIA
text: No, Cassio is not killed.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Not Cassio killed? Then murder’s out of tune,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And sweet revenge grows harsh.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: O falsely, falsely murdered.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O Lord, what cry is that?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That? What?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Out, and alas, that was my lady’s voice!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Help! Help ho! Help! O lady, speak again!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Sweet Desdemona, O sweet mistress, speak!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: A guiltless death I die.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, who hath done this deed?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Nobody. I myself. Farewell.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Commend me to my kind lord. O, farewell.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why, how should she be murdered?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Alas, who knows?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: You heard her say herself, it was not I.

speaker: EMILIA
text: She said so. I must needs report the truth.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She’s like a liar gone to burning hell!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Twas I that killed her.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, the more angel she, and you the blacker devil!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She turned to folly, and she was a whore.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Thou dost belie her, and thou art a devil!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She was false as water.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Thou art rash as fire to say

speaker: EMILIA
text: That she was false. O, she was heavenly true!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Cassio did top her. Ask thy husband else.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, I were damned beneath all depth in hell

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But that I did proceed upon just grounds

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To this extremity. Thy husband knew it all.

speaker: EMILIA
text: My husband?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thy husband.

speaker: EMILIA
text: That she was false to wedlock?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, with Cassio. Had she been true,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If heaven would make me such another world

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of one entire and perfect chrysolite,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’d not have sold her for it.

speaker: EMILIA
text: My husband?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, ’twas he that told me on her first.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: An honest man he is, and hates the slime

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That sticks on filthy deeds.

speaker: EMILIA
text: My husband?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What needs this iterance, woman? I say, thy husband.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O mistress, villainy hath made mocks with love!—

speaker: EMILIA
text: My husband say she was false?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: He, woman.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I say thy husband. Dost understand the word?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: My friend, thy husband; honest, honest Iago.

speaker: EMILIA
text: If he say so, may his pernicious soul

speaker: EMILIA
text: Rot half a grain a day! He lies to th’ heart!

speaker: EMILIA
text: She was too fond of her most filthy bargain.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Hah?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Do thy worst!

speaker: EMILIA
text: This deed of thine is no more worthy heaven

speaker: EMILIA
text: Than thou wast worthy her.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Peace, you were best!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Thou hast not half that power to do me harm

speaker: EMILIA
text: As I have to be hurt. O gull! O dolt,

speaker: EMILIA
text: As ignorant as dirt! Thou hast done a deed—

speaker: EMILIA
text: I care not for thy sword. I’ll make thee known,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Though I lost twenty lives. Help! Help, ho! Help!

speaker: EMILIA
text: The Moor hath killed my mistress! Murder, murder!

speaker: MONTANO
text: What is the matter? How now, general?

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, are you come, Iago? You have done well,

speaker: EMILIA
text: That men must lay their murders on your neck.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: What is the matter?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Disprove this villain, if thou be’st a man.

speaker: EMILIA
text: He says thou told’st him that his wife was false.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I know thou didst not. Thou ’rt not such a villain.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Speak, for my heart is full.

speaker: IAGO
text: I told him what I thought, and told no more

speaker: IAGO
text: Than what he found himself was apt and true.

speaker: EMILIA
text: But did you ever tell him she was false?

speaker: IAGO
text: I did.

speaker: EMILIA
text: You told a lie, an odious, damnèd lie!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Upon my soul, a lie, a wicked lie!

speaker: EMILIA
text: She false with Cassio? Did you say with Cassio?

speaker: IAGO
text: With Cassio, mistress. Go to! Charm your tongue.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I will not charm my tongue. I am bound to speak.

speaker: EMILIA
text: My mistress here lies murdered in her bed.

speaker: ALL
text: O heavens forfend!

speaker: EMILIA
text: And your reports have set the murder on!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nay, stare not, masters; it is true indeed.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: ’Tis a strange truth.

speaker: MONTANO
text: O monstrous act!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Villainy, villainy, villainy!

speaker: EMILIA
text: I think upon ’t, I think! I smell ’t! O villainy!

speaker: EMILIA
text: I thought so then. I’ll kill myself for grief!

speaker: EMILIA
text: O villainy! Villainy!

speaker: IAGO
text: What, are you mad? I charge you get you home.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Good gentlemen, let me have leave to speak.

speaker: EMILIA
text: ’Tis proper I obey him, but not now.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Perchance, Iago, I will ne’er go home.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, O, O!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Nay, lay thee down, and roar!

speaker: EMILIA
text: For thou hast killed the sweetest innocent

speaker: EMILIA
text: That e’er did lift up eye.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, she was foul!—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I scarce did know you, uncle. There lies your niece,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Whose breath indeed these hands have newly stopped.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I know this act shows horrible and grim.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Poor Desdemon, I am glad thy father’s dead.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Thy match was mortal to him, and pure grief

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Shore his old thread in twain. Did he live now,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: This sight would make him do a desperate turn,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Yea, curse his better angel from his side,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: And fall to reprobance.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis pitiful. But yet Iago knows

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That she with Cassio hath the act of shame

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A thousand times committed. Cassio confessed it,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And she did gratify his amorous works

speaker: OTHELLO
text: With that recognizance and pledge of love

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Which I first gave her. I saw it in his hand.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It was a handkerchief, an antique token

speaker: OTHELLO
text: My father gave my mother.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O God! O heavenly God!

speaker: IAGO
text: Zounds, hold your peace!

speaker: EMILIA
text: ’Twill out, ’twill out. I peace?

speaker: EMILIA
text: No, I will speak as liberal as the north.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Let heaven and men and devils, let them all,

speaker: EMILIA
text: All, all, cry shame against me, yet I’ll speak.

speaker: IAGO
text: Be wise, and get you home.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I will not.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Fie, your sword upon a woman!

speaker: EMILIA
text: O thou dull Moor, that handkerchief thou speak’st of

speaker: EMILIA
text: I found by fortune, and did give my husband—

speaker: EMILIA
text: For often, with a solemn earnestness

speaker: EMILIA
text: (More than indeed belonged to such a trifle),

speaker: EMILIA
text: He begged of me to steal ’t.

speaker: IAGO
text: Villainous whore!

speaker: EMILIA
text: She give it Cassio? No, alas, I found it,

speaker: EMILIA
text: And I did give ’t my husband.

speaker: IAGO
text: Filth, thou liest!

speaker: EMILIA
text: By heaven, I do not, I do not, gentlemen!

speaker: EMILIA
text:
To
                 
                Othello
                .
                 
              O murd’rous coxcomb, what should such a fool

speaker: EMILIA
text: Do with so good a wife?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Are there no stones in heaven

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But what serves for the thunder?—Precious villain!

speaker: GRATIANO
text: The woman falls! Sure he hath killed his wife.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Ay, ay! O, lay me by my mistress’ side.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: He’s gone, but his wife’s killed.

speaker: MONTANO
text: ’Tis a notorious villain. Take you this weapon

speaker: MONTANO
text: Which I have here recovered from the Moor.

speaker: MONTANO
text: Come, guard the door without. Let him not pass,

speaker: MONTANO
text: But kill him rather. I’ll after that same villain,

speaker: MONTANO
text: For ’tis a damnèd slave.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I am not valiant neither,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But every puny whipster gets my sword.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But why should honor outlive honesty?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let it go all.

speaker: EMILIA
text: What did thy song bode, lady?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Hark, canst thou hear me? I will play the swan

speaker: EMILIA
text: And die in music.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Willow, willow, willow.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Moor, she was chaste. She loved thee, cruel Moor.

speaker: EMILIA
text: So come my soul to bliss, as I speak true.

speaker: EMILIA
text: So speaking as I think, alas, I die.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I have another weapon in this chamber.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: It is a sword of Spain, the ice brook’s temper.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, here it is.—Uncle, I must come forth.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: If thou attempt it, it will cost thee dear;

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Thou hast no weapon and perforce must suffer.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Look in upon me, then, and speak with me,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Or naked as I am I will assault thee.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: What is the matter?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Behold, I have a weapon.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A better never did itself sustain

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Upon a soldier’s thigh. I have seen the day

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That with this little arm and this good sword

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I have made my way through more impediments

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Than twenty times your stop. But—O vain boast!—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Who can control his fate? ’Tis not so now.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Be not afraid, though you do see me weaponed.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Here is my journey’s end, here is my butt

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And very sea-mark of my utmost sail.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Do you go back dismayed? ’Tis a lost fear.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Man but a rush against Othello’s breast,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And he retires. Where should Othello go?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Now, how dost thou look now? O ill-starred wench,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Pale as thy smock, when we shall meet at compt,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: This look of thine will hurl my soul from heaven,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And fiends will snatch at it. Cold, cold, my girl?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Even like thy chastity.—O cursèd, cursèd slave!—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Whip me, you devils,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: From the possession of this heavenly sight!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Blow me about in winds, roast me in sulfur,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Wash me in steep-down gulfs of liquid fire!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O Desdemon! Dead, Desdemon! Dead! O, O!

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Where is this rash and most unfortunate man?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That’s he that was Othello. Here I am.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Where is that viper? Bring the villain forth.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I look down towards his feet; but that’s a fable.—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If that thou be’st a devil, I cannot kill thee.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Wrench his sword from him.

speaker: IAGO
text: I bleed, sir, but not killed.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I am not sorry neither. I’d have thee live,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For in my sense ’tis happiness to die.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: O thou Othello, that wert once so good,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Fall’n in the practice of a damnèd slave,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: What shall be said to thee?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why, anything.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: An honorable murderer, if you will,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For naught I did in hate, but all in honor.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: This wretch hath part confessed his villainy.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Did you and he consent in Cassio’s death?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Dear general, I never gave you cause.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I do believe it, and I ask your pardon.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Will you, I pray, demand that demi-devil

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why he hath thus ensnared my soul and body?

speaker: IAGO
text: Demand me nothing. What you know, you know.

speaker: IAGO
text: From this time forth I never will speak word.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: What? Not to pray?

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Torments will ope your lips.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Well, thou dost best.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Sir,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: You shall understand what hath befall’n,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Which, as I think, you know not. Here is a letter

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Found in the pocket of the slain Roderigo,

speaker: LODOVICO
text:
And here another.

                shows
                 
                papers

text: The death of Cassio, to be undertook

text: O villain!

speaker: CASSIO
text: Most heathenish and most gross.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Now here’s another discontented paper

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Found in his pocket, too; and this it seems

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Roderigo meant t’ have sent this damnèd villain,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: But that, belike, Iago in the interim

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Came in and satisfied him.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, thou pernicious caitiff!—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: How came you, Cassio, by that handkerchief

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That was my wife’s?

speaker: CASSIO
text: I found it in my chamber.

speaker: CASSIO
text: And he himself confessed it but even now,

speaker: CASSIO
text: That there he dropped it for a special purpose

speaker: CASSIO
text: Which wrought to his desire.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O fool, fool, fool!

speaker: CASSIO
text: There is besides, in Roderigo’s letter,

speaker: CASSIO
text: How he upbraids Iago, that he made him

speaker: CASSIO
text: Brave me upon the watch, whereon it came

speaker: CASSIO
text: That I was cast. And even but now he spake,

speaker: CASSIO
text: After long seeming dead: Iago hurt him,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Iago set him on.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: You must forsake this room and go with us.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Your power and your command is taken off,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: And Cassio rules in Cyprus. For this slave,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: If there be any cunning cruelty

speaker: LODOVICO
text: That can torment him much and hold him long,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: It shall be his. You shall close prisoner rest,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Till that the nature of your fault be known

speaker: LODOVICO
text: To the Venetian state.—Come, bring away.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Soft you. A word or two before you go.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I have done the state some service, and they know ’t.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: No more of that. I pray you in your letters,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: When you shall these unlucky deeds relate,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Speak of me as I am. Nothing extenuate,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nor set down aught in malice. Then must you speak

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of one that loved not wisely, but too well;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of one not easily jealous, but being wrought,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Perplexed in the extreme; of one whose hand,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Like the base Judean, threw a pearl away

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Richer than all his tribe; of one whose subdued eyes,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Albeit unused to the melting mood,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Drops tears as fast as the Arabian trees

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Their medicinable gum. Set you down this.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And say besides, that in Aleppo once,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Where a malignant and a turbanned Turk

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Beat a Venetian and traduced the state,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I took by th’ throat the circumcisèd dog,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And smote him, thus.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: O bloody period!

speaker: GRATIANO
text: All that is spoke is marred.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I kissed thee ere I killed thee. No way but this,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Killing myself, to die upon a kiss.

speaker: CASSIO
text: This did I fear, but thought he had no weapon,

speaker: CASSIO
text: For he was great of heart.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: O Spartan dog,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: More fell than anguish, hunger, or the sea,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Look on the tragic loading of this bed.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: This is thy work.—The object poisons sight.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Let it be hid.—Gratiano, keep the house,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: And seize upon the fortunes of the Moor,

speaker: LODOVICO
text:
For they succeed on you.
                 
                To
                 
                Cassio
                .
               To you, lord governor,

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Remains the censure of this hellish villain.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: The time, the place, the torture, O, enforce it.

speaker: LODOVICO
text: Myself will straight aboard, and to the state

speaker: LODOVICO
text: This heavy act with heavy heart relate.
